examine.tree: A sturdy old tree. The birds know its branches better than you do.
examine.bush: A tangle of leaves. Something small rustles within, then thinks better of it.
examine.rock: A weathered grey rock. It has outlasted every king who ever cursed it.
examine.oak: A broad-shouldered oak. Strong wood for those patient enough to fell it.
examine.willow: A weeping willow, trailing its branches like a mourner at the water's edge.
examine.fishing-spot: The water ripples. Something with fins is going about its day down there.
examine.fire: A crackling fire. Warm hands, warm hearts, singed eyebrows if you lean in too close.
examine.range: A cooking range, blackened with the labour of a thousand suppers.
examine.furnace: A roaring furnace. Hot enough to coax metal out of stubborn ore.
examine.anvil: A scarred iron anvil. It has rung out more songs than any minstrel.
examine.altar: A quiet altar. Whispered prayers seem to gather here like dust.
examine.bank-booth: A bank booth. Your worldly goods sleep safely behind it.
examine.poll-booth: A polling booth, where the folk of Eldermoor make their voices heard.
examine.ladder-down: A ladder leading down into the dark. Mind the splinters.
examine.ladder-up: A ladder leading up toward the light. The rungs creak a warning.
examine.door: A plain wooden door. It keeps the weather out and the secrets in.
examine.gate: A creaking gate. It swings on hinges that have forgotten the meaning of oil.
examine.fence: A row of weathered fence posts, holding the line against wandering livestock.
examine.wall: A solid stone wall. Generations of moss have made it their home.
examine.window: A small window, its glass warped and dim with age.
examine.pew: A worn wooden pew, polished smooth by countless faithful backsides.
examine.banner: A hanging banner, its colours faded but its pride undimmed.
examine.candle: A lone candle, holding back the dark one flickering inch at a time.
examine.organ: A grand pipe organ. You can almost hear the hymns sleeping in its pipes.
examine.pond: A still pond. Frogs hold court here on warm evenings.
examine.water: Clear water, cool and uncomplaining. It asks nothing and gives much.
examine.sea: The restless sea, stretching out to where the world simply gives up.
examine.dirt-path: A well-trodden dirt path. Countless boots have worn it into the earth.
examine.grass: Soft green grass. It does not care who walks upon it.
examine.sign: A weathered signpost, pointing the way for those who can still read it.
examine.boat: A modest little boat, bobbing patiently at its mooring.
examine.chicken: A plump chicken, strutting about as though it owns the whole farmyard.
examine.rat: A scrawny rat. It eyes you with the bold contempt of a born survivor.
examine.well: A deep stone well. Drop a coin and the dark swallows your wish whole.
examine.crate: A wooden crate. Whatever it held has long since gone elsewhere.
examine.barrel: A stout wooden barrel. It smells faintly of whatever once sloshed within.
examine.sack: A coarse cloth sack, slumped and lumpy with its quiet cargo.
examine.table: A sturdy table, scarred by years of meals, elbows, and idle daggers.
examine.chair: A simple wooden chair. It has held many a weary traveller.
examine.bed: A humble bed. It promises the one luxury that gold cannot buy: rest.
examine.bookshelf: A dusty bookshelf, sagging beneath the weight of forgotten knowledge.
examine.lectern: A reading lectern, worn where a hundred fingers have followed the words.
examine.sconce: A wall sconce, its flame keeping the shadows politely at bay.
